content.content:
1	# RFQ Module
2	
3	This is the primary feature module. It manages vendor requests-for-quote and computes the full cost chain from FOB factory price to retail sell price.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## File map
8	
9	| File | Role |
10	|---|---|
11	| `rfq.component.ts` | Master grid component. AG Grid setup, column wiring (valueSetters/valueGetters), dialog launches, row save/delete. ~4,100 lines. |
12	| `helpers/equation.ts` | All margin and sell-price calculations. Injectable service (`rfqEquation`). No AG Grid imports — pure business math. |
13	| `helpers/api-error.ts` | HTTP error message extraction (shared across the app, lives here for historical reasons). |
14	| `helpers/price-sales-snapshot.ts` | Pure helpers (no AG Grid imports, unit-tested): `capturePriceSalesSnapshot()` snapshots duty rate + container price when a "Price for Sales" cell is edited; `buildSnapshotTooltip()` formats the tooltip text. |
15	| `price-sales-snapshot-renderer/` | Cell renderer for the 8 `*_price_sales` columns; renders the currency value and a native `title` hover tooltip built from the row's `price_sales_snapshots`. |
16	| `detail-cell-renderer/` | AG Grid master-detail renderer for vendor quotes. Each RFQ item expands to show its vendor rows. |
17	| `addNewProduct/` | Dialog for adding a new RFQ item (full form with all fields). |
18	| `addNewProductGrid/` | Alternative add-product flow using a mini-grid inside a dialog. |
19	| `duplicate_dialog/` | Duplicates an existing RFQ item (optionally copies vendors). |
20	| `container_cost_dialog/` | Dialog to set container cost (USD/container); triggers freight and landed-cost recalculation. |
21	| `duty_rate_dialog/` | Dialog to set the duty rate. |
22	| `multi_quote/` | Dialog to send quote requests to multiple vendors at once. |
23	| `autocomplete/` | Custom AG Grid cell editor for fields with server-loaded dropdown options. |
24	| `custom-dropdown/` | AG Grid cell editor for RFQ Group selection with add-new inline capability. |
25	| `numericEditor/` | AG Grid cell editor for numeric fields (validates > 0, < 1,000,000). |
26	| `datepicker/` | AG Grid cell editor for date fields. |
27	| `layout-dropdown/` | Toolbar component for saving/loading named grid view layouts. |
28	| `newVendor/` | Dialog to create a new vendor and assign it to an RFQ item. |
29	| `vendor/` | "Select Factories" dialog — attach one or more factories to an RFQ item. Factories already on the item are shown red + unclickable so staff can't add a duplicate (see "Detail grid" below). |
30	
31	Grid column definitions live in **`src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq.grid.config.ts`**, not in this folder.
32	
33	---
34	
35	## Key concept: `getMasterFiledName(colId)`
36	
37	Every piece of code that reads or writes to a row's data must go through `getMasterFiledName(colId)` to get the actual storage field name. This function iterates `this.colHeaders` (the column headers loaded from the backend) and returns the `field` value for a given `colId`.
38	
39	- `colId` = stable lowercase-underscore identifier (e.g. `"fob_cost"`, `"cbm_pc"`)
40	- `field` = the backend storage key, which may contain spaces or special chars (e.g. `"FOB Cost"`, `"CBM/pc"`)
41	
42	**Note:** The function name has a typo (`Filed` instead of `Field`). This is preserved intentionally — see AGENTS.md §11.
43	
44	In `equation.ts`, the same function takes `colHeaders` as a second parameter: `getMasterFiledName(colId, colHeaders)`.
45	
46	---
47	
48	## Calculation pipeline
49	
50	**For all pricing formulas, INCOTERM cost bases, deduction logic, and scenarios see [`docs/rfq-math.md`](../../../../docs/rfq-math.md). Only read that file when working on math — skip it otherwise.**
51	
52	### Recalculation trigger chain
53	
54	When a sourcing input is edited, the valueSetter in `rfq.component.ts` calls:
55	
56	```
57	calculateLandedCost()        ← only if fob_cost / agent / duty / freight changed
58	recalculate_Lic_Gen()
59	  → setGenFobMargin          → gen_fob_sell_price
60	  → setGenMddpMargin         → gen_mddp_sell_price
61	  → setGenPoeMargin          → gen_poe_sell_price
62	  → setGenWhseMargin         → gen_whse_sell_price
63	  → setLicFobMargin          → lic_fob_sell_price
64	  → setLicMddpMargin         → lic_mddp_sell_price
65	  → setLicPoeMargin          → lic_poe_sell_price
66	  → setLicWhseMargin         → lic_whse_sell_price
67	```
68	
69	All 8 setters run on every triggering edit to keep all columns in sync. Sell-price setters are only triggered when the user directly edits a sell-price cell.
70	
71	---
72	
73	## Price for Sales snapshot tooltips
74	
75	The 8 `*_price_sales` columns are manually entered (the price sent to the sales team). A price-for-sales value is only meaningful relative to the **Duty Rate %** and **Container Price** at the time it was set — both of which drift. So when one of these cells is edited, `onCellValueChanged` calls `capturePriceSalesSnapshot()` (in `helpers/price-sales-snapshot.ts`), which records `{ duty_rate, duty_dollar, container_price, at }` into `params.data.price_sales_snapshots[colId]`. Clearing the cell removes that entry. The snapshot is persisted by the existing `updateRfqItem(params.data)` call (backend column `rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots`, TEXT/JSON).
76	
77	The columns render through `priceSalesSnapshotRenderer`, which sets a native `title` tooltip from the stored snapshot so it always shows on hover. Rows with no recorded snapshot (legacy) show no tooltip. See AGENTS.md §11 for why a native tooltip is used here instead of the shared `customTooltip`.
78	
79	---
80	
81	## AG Grid patterns specific to this module
82	
83	### Active status blockers and bilingual feedback
84	
85	What changed:
86	Changing an RFQ row from Draft or Inactive to Active is blocked until every required launch field is present: Tech Pack Link, Case Pack, RFQ Group, Quantity, Customer, License, Description, and Delivery Location. `rfq.component.ts` passes the runtime field names from `getMasterFiledName()` into `helpers/rfq-status-validation.ts`; the validator returns `missingFields`, and both the snackbar and Status-cell native `title` tooltip show the exact missing list in English and Simplified Mandarin.
87	
88	Why:
89	Users were blocked with a Tech Pack-only message even when another field was missing. The RFQ grid field names come from backend layout data, so the validator must receive runtime field names instead of hardcoding display labels.
90	
91	Future sessions should:
92	When adding or removing Active-status prerequisites, update `getActiveStatusRequiredFields()` in `rfq.component.ts` and `rfq-status-validation.spec.ts`. Do not add a second ad hoc snackbar for one field; keep the missing-field list centralized so hover and click feedback stay identical.
93	
94	### Silent blockers must explain the reason
95	
96	What changed:
97	Common RFQ disabled/failure paths now surface bilingual feedback instead of only `console.error`: copy/new-round RFQ creation, vendor decline / Keep Price, factory bulk offer upload, and add-product option-load failures.
98	
99	Why:
100	Production/vendor users can be blocked by missing group names, missing `.xlsx` files, inactive merch groups, failed dropdown loads, or backend copy guards. Console-only errors are invisible in normal use, and disabled Material buttons often need a wrapper element for tooltips to fire.
101	
102	Future sessions should:
103	For any new disabled RFQ action, add a tooltip that names the missing prerequisite. For any caught API failure that changes user workflow, keep the `console.error` but also show a concise snackbar. If the disabled element is a Material button, wrap it in an inline element with `matTooltip`.
104	
105	### Async valueSetters
106	
107	Every valueSetter that calls `recalculate_Lic_Gen` is `async`. AG Grid v35 awaits the returned Promise — a rejection cancels the cell edit and reverts the value. Always:
108	- Return `true` on success
109	- Return `false` and show a snackbar on validation failure
110	- Do not let any exception escape (the try-catch in `recalculate_Lic_Gen` itself covers margin errors, but the outer setter must also be safe)
111	
112	### Fill handle: keep drag-copy, block double-click fill-down
113	
114	What changed:
115	The master RFQ grid still enables AG Grid fill-handle drag-copy through `cellSelection.handle: { mode: 'fill', direction: 'y', setFillValue }`. `rfq.component.ts` also installs `installFillHandleDoubleClickBlocker()` on grid ready, which catches `dblclick` events on `.ag-fill-handle` during the capture phase and stops AG Grid from auto-filling down the grid.
116	
117	Why:
118	Double-clicking the selected-cell fill handle copied a Tech Pack Link value down the displayed grid and destroyed many row values. Users still need normal drag-to-copy, so removing the fill handle is not acceptable.
119	
120	Future sessions should:
121	Do not "fix" this by setting `suppressFillHandle: true` globally or removing `cellSelection.handle`; that breaks drag-copy. AG Grid v35.1 docs expose the fill handle as an on/off feature and do not document a narrower double-click-only setting, so keep the event blocker unless AG Grid adds a first-class option. Verify with `rfq.component.spec.ts` tests for drag fill and double-click blocking.
122	
123	### Tech Pack Link editing and opening
124	
125	What changed:
126	The Tech Pack Link column renders through `renderTechPackCell()` instead of returning a raw `<a>` string. The cell shows the URL text plus explicit `Edit`, `Open`, and `Upload` buttons; normal clicks no longer navigate away.
127	
128	Why:
129	When the whole cell was an anchor, users could not reliably select/edit/delete URL text because clicking the cell opened a new browser tab. The explicit buttons keep opening/uploading available while leaving AG Grid text editing accessible via double-click or the `Edit` button.
130	
131	Future sessions should:
132	Keep Tech Pack Link navigation behind an explicit control. If changing this renderer, verify that users can still edit/delete the text value and that opening the link requires clicking `Open`.
133	
134	### Native AG Grid undo
135	
136	What changed:
137	RFQ uses AG Grid's native undo stack (`undoRedoCellEditing: true`, limit `50`), and the toolbar Undo button calls `gridApi.undoCellEditing()`. The old custom undo path that fetched all RFQ rows on every edit was removed.
138	
139	Why:
140	AG Grid records edits, fill-handle operations, paste, delete, undo, and redo through `cellValueChanged`; the custom snapshot restore did not reliably cover fill/copy/paste and could restore stale server data.
141	
142	Future sessions should:
143	Keep persistence in `onCellValueChanged` so undo operations save the reverted row. If adding custom edit flows, make sure they use normal AG Grid value changes (`setDataValue`, valueSetter, or editor commit) so the undo stack can see them.
144	
145	### Column identity: never hardcode field names
146	
147	```typescript
148	// Correct
149	params.data[this.getMasterFiledName('cbm_pc')]
150	
151	// Wrong
152	params.data['CBM/pc']
153	```
154	
155	### Duty rate: always use optional chaining
156	
157	The duty rate can be stored as `{ title: "7.5" }` (object from autocomplete) or as a raw numeric string. Always:
158	
159	```typescript
160	const dutyRateRaw = data[this.getMasterFiledName('duty_rate')];
161	const duty_rate = dutyRateRaw?.title ?? dutyRateRaw ?? 0;
162	```
163	
164	### cellDataType: false on numeric string columns
165	
166	Any column that uses `numericCellEditor` AND whose backend data is stored as strings **must** have `cellDataType: false` in its runtime setup block. Without it, AG Grid v35 infers the column type as `'text'` and issues warning `#135`, silently reverting the edit before the valueSetter is called.
167	
168	```typescript
169	} else if (colHeaders[i].colId == "cbm_pc") {
170	  colHeaders[i].cellDataType = false;        // ← prevents warning #135
171	  colHeaders[i]['cellEditor'] = 'numericCellEditor';
172	  colHeaders[i].valueSetter = async (params) => { ... };
173	}
174	```
175	
176	Do NOT use `cellDataType: 'number'` — that applies AG Grid's built-in number formatter and shows "invalid number" for string-valued cells loaded from the backend.
177	
178	The `numericCellEditor` already enforces number-only input via `getValue()` returning `null` for any non-parseable value, so `cellDataType: false` does not open the door to bad data.
179	
180	### AG Grid deprecated APIs — what NOT to use
181	
182	| Removed | Replacement | Removed in |
183	|---|---|---|
184	| `params.column.userProvidedColDef` | `params.column.getColDef()` | AG Grid v35 |
185	| `params.columnApi` | `params.api` | AG Grid v31 |
186	| `getRowNodeId` | `getRowId` | AG Grid v28 |
187	| `observable.toPromise()` | `firstValueFrom(observable)` | RxJS 7 (deprecated) |
188	
189	---
190	
191	## Detail grid (vendor quotes)
192	
193	Each RFQ item row expands to show vendor quotes in a detail grid managed by `detail-cell-renderer.component.ts`. The detail grid:
194	
195	- Loads its own column definitions from the backend (`getGridHeader({ grid_id: 'rfq_detail' })`)
196	- Has its own `onCellValueChanged` that calls `updateVendor(data)` on every change
197	- Uses `addDetailGridInfo` / `removeDetailGridInfo` for master-detail grid registration
198	- The `price_code` column change in the detail grid sets `quote_accepted = true` on the vendor row
199	- Default-sorts by factory price ascending — see "Default sort" below
200	
201	### Default sort: lowest price first, unpriced/declined last
202	
203	`configureDetailColumnHeaders()` calls `applyDefaultPriceSort(colHeaders)` after building the
204	column defs. This makes the **price column the primary default sort** (`sort: 'asc'`,
205	`sortIndex: 0`); the `name` column stays as the secondary alphabetical tiebreaker (`sortIndex: 1`).
206	
207	A custom comparator on the price column ranks rows so that any quote with no usable price
208	(blank / zero / negative `Price`) or where the factory **declined to quote** (`req_status == 1`,
209	set by the "pass" button in `rfq-button.component.ts`) is sunk to the bottom regardless of value;
210	real quotes are ordered lowest-price-first above them. The comparator reads `node.data.Price`
211	directly (via `isUnpricedOrDeclined()` / `toPrice()`), so it does not depend on the column's
212	`colId`, `field`, or `valueGetter`.
213	
214	**Why `applyDefaultPriceSort` searches for the colId instead of hardcoding it:** the backend
215	`rfq_detail` layout serves the visible price column under colId **`price`** (legacy), while the
216	config file `rfq.detail.grid.config.ts` uses `quote_price`. The helper targets whichever of the
217	two the layout actually provides, preferring the visible one. The first attempt (`e8ba0551`)
218	hardcoded `quote_price` and silently did nothing because the live column is `price`; fixed in
219	`b241ce79`. See `AGENTS.md §11 → "Detail-grid default sort targets the live price colId, not a hardcoded one"`.
220	
221	### Vendor quote rows and the duplicate-factory guard
222	
223	Each item's quotes live on the master row's **`Vendors`** array (capital V — Sequelize association name); each element is `{ Vendor: { id, ... }, Price, Note, Status, quote_id, ... }`. The same `Vendor.id` may appear more than once:
224	
225	- **Allowed (factory side):** the detail sub-grid "Add Quote" button lets a factory submit a second quote line for the item, as long as a note explains why the price differs. This path is intentionally **unguarded**.
226	- **Blocked (staff side):** the "Add Factory" → "Select Factories" dialog (`vendor/`) must not let sourcing/production/admin add a factory that is already on the item. `onBtnNewVendor()` builds `existingVendorIds` from `node.data.Vendors.map(v => v?.Vendor?.id)` and passes them as `DialogData.vendors`; the dialog renders those factories red and unclickable.
227	
228	The guard is **UI-only** — there is no DB unique constraint or backend dedupe on `(RFQ item, vendor)`, and it depends on the exact capital-`V` `Vendors` casing. See `AGENTS.md §11 → "Same factory twice on one RFQ item"`.
229	
230	### Re-quote a stale price (orange flag + Keep Price)
231	
232	When a factory's price is old, internal Sourcing/admin can right-click the detail-grid price cell → **"Request new quote"** (`getContextMenuItems`, gated to `admin`/`sourcing_manager`/`production`). That calls `itemService.requestRequote({ quote_id, priceCode })`, which sets `RFQVendor.requote_requested = true` and emails the factory. The flag drives, in **both** the staff and vendor views:
233	
234	- an **orange** price cell — `cellClassRules: { 'requote-requested-cell': … }`, added to **both** the `quote_price` and legacy `price` colId branches (the live layout may serve either — same reason as the default-sort helper above);
235	- a **bilingual tooltip** `报价 - Quoted <Month D YYYY>` via `tooltipValueGetter`, using the original `quote_date` (which `requestRequote` leaves untouched).
236	
237	The vendor clears it two ways: typing a **new price** (the `quote_price` valueSetter sets `requote_requested = false` locally; backend clears it on save) or clicking the **保留价格 - Keep Price** button (`rfq-button.component.ts` `onKeepPrice()` → `itemService.keepPrice({ quote_id })`, which clears the flag and refreshes `quote_date` to today). Vendor-facing strings are inline `中文 - English` (no i18n framework — see `AGENTS.md §11`). The backend guards both mutating endpoints with `vendorMayModifyQuote` so a vendor can't act on another factory's quote via `quote_id`.
238	
239	---
240	
241	## Window globals (known tech debt)
242	
243	`window['itemService']` references in toolbar callbacks were resolved in commit `40b3ac9` — the service is now captured at closure-creation time in `getContextMenuItems()`.
244	
245	Remaining `window` globals in `rfq.component.ts` (`window['router']`, `window['gridApi']`, `window['base64Images']`, `window['togglePageToolbar']`) are used for inter-callback communication in contexts where Angular DI is not available. **Do not add new `window['...']` references.**
246

file.content:
# RFQ Module

This is the primary feature module. It manages vendor requests-for-quote and computes the full cost chain from FOB factory price to retail sell price.

---

## File map

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `rfq.component.ts` | Master grid component. AG Grid setup, column wiring (valueSetters/valueGetters), dialog launches, row save/delete. ~4,100 lines. |
| `helpers/equation.ts` | All margin and sell-price calculations. Injectable service (`rfqEquation`). No AG Grid imports — pure business math. |
| `helpers/api-error.ts` | HTTP error message extraction (shared across the app, lives here for historical reasons). |
| `helpers/price-sales-snapshot.ts` | Pure helpers (no AG Grid imports, unit-tested): `capturePriceSalesSnapshot()` snapshots duty rate + container price when a "Price for Sales" cell is edited; `buildSnapshotTooltip()` formats the tooltip text. |
| `price-sales-snapshot-renderer/` | Cell renderer for the 8 `*_price_sales` columns; renders the currency value and a native `title` hover tooltip built from the row's `price_sales_snapshots`. |
| `detail-cell-renderer/` | AG Grid master-detail renderer for vendor quotes. Each RFQ item expands to show its vendor rows. |
| `addNewProduct/` | Dialog for adding a new RFQ item (full form with all fields). |
| `addNewProductGrid/` | Alternative add-product flow using a mini-grid inside a dialog. |
| `duplicate_dialog/` | Duplicates an existing RFQ item (optionally copies vendors). |
| `container_cost_dialog/` | Dialog to set container cost (USD/container); triggers freight and landed-cost recalculation. |
| `duty_rate_dialog/` | Dialog to set the duty rate. |
| `multi_quote/` | Dialog to send quote requests to multiple vendors at once. |
| `autocomplete/` | Custom AG Grid cell editor for fields with server-loaded dropdown options. |
| `custom-dropdown/` | AG Grid cell editor for RFQ Group selection with add-new inline capability. |
| `numericEditor/` | AG Grid cell editor for numeric fields (validates > 0, < 1,000,000). |
| `datepicker/` | AG Grid cell editor for date fields. |
| `layout-dropdown/` | Toolbar component for saving/loading named grid view layouts. |
| `newVendor/` | Dialog to create a new vendor and assign it to an RFQ item. |
| `vendor/` | "Select Factories" dialog — attach one or more factories to an RFQ item. Factories already on the item are shown red + unclickable so staff can't add a duplicate (see "Detail grid" below). |

Grid column definitions live in **`src/app/helpers/ag-grid/rfq-grid/rfq.grid.config.ts`**, not in this folder.

---

## Key concept: `getMasterFiledName(colId)`

Every piece of code that reads or writes to a row's data must go through `getMasterFiledName(colId)` to get the actual storage field name. This function iterates `this.colHeaders` (the column headers loaded from the backend) and returns the `field` value for a given `colId`.

- `colId` = stable lowercase-underscore identifier (e.g. `"fob_cost"`, `"cbm_pc"`)
- `field` = the backend storage key, which may contain spaces or special chars (e.g. `"FOB Cost"`, `"CBM/pc"`)

**Note:** The function name has a typo (`Filed` instead of `Field`). This is preserved intentionally — see AGENTS.md §11.

In `equation.ts`, the same function takes `colHeaders` as a second parameter: `getMasterFiledName(colId, colHeaders)`.

---

## Calculation pipeline

**For all pricing formulas, INCOTERM cost bases, deduction logic, and scenarios see [`docs/rfq-math.md`](../../../../docs/rfq-math.md). Only read that file when working on math — skip it otherwise.**

### Recalculation trigger chain

When a sourcing input is edited, the valueSetter in `rfq.component.ts` calls:

```
calculateLandedCost()        ← only if fob_cost / agent / duty / freight changed
recalculate_Lic_Gen()
  → setGenFobMargin          → gen_fob_sell_price
  → setGenMddpMargin         → gen_mddp_sell_price
  → setGenPoeMargin          → gen_poe_sell_price
  → setGenWhseMargin         → gen_whse_sell_price
  → setLicFobMargin          → lic_fob_sell_price
  → setLicMddpMargin         → lic_mddp_sell_price
  → setLicPoeMargin          → lic_poe_sell_price
  → setLicWhseMargin         → lic_whse_sell_price
```

All 8 setters run on every triggering edit to keep all columns in sync. Sell-price setters are only triggered when the user directly edits a sell-price cell.

---

## Price for Sales snapshot tooltips

The 8 `*_price_sales` columns are manually entered (the price sent to the sales team). A price-for-sales value is only meaningful relative to the **Duty Rate %** and **Container Price** at the time it was set — both of which drift. So when one of these cells is edited, `onCellValueChanged` calls `capturePriceSalesSnapshot()` (in `helpers/price-sales-snapshot.ts`), which records `{ duty_rate, duty_dollar, container_price, at }` into `params.data.price_sales_snapshots[colId]`. Clearing the cell removes that entry. The snapshot is persisted by the existing `updateRfqItem(params.data)` call (backend column `rfqItem_price_sales_snapshots`, TEXT/JSON).

The columns render through `priceSalesSnapshotRenderer`, which sets a native `title` tooltip from the stored snapshot so it always shows on hover. Rows with no recorded snapshot (legacy) show no tooltip. See AGENTS.md §11 for why a native tooltip is used here instead of the shared `customTooltip`.

---

## AG Grid patterns specific to this module

### Active status blockers and bilingual feedback

What changed:
Changing an RFQ row from Draft or Inactive to Active is blocked until every required launch field is present: Tech Pack Link, Case Pack, RFQ Group, Quantity, Customer, License, Description, and Delivery Location. `rfq.component.ts` passes the runtime field names from `getMasterFiledName()` into `helpers/rfq-status-validation.ts`; the validator returns `missingFields`, and both the snackbar and Status-cell native `title` tooltip show the exact missing list in English and Simplified Mandarin.

Why:
Users were blocked with a Tech Pack-only message even when another field was missing. The RFQ grid field names come from backend layout data, so the validator must receive runtime field names instead of hardcoding display labels.

Future sessions should:
When adding or removing Active-status prerequisites, update `getActiveStatusRequiredFields()` in `rfq.component.ts` and `rfq-status-validation.spec.ts`. Do not add a second ad hoc snackbar for one field; keep the missing-field list centralized so hover and click feedback stay identical.

### Silent blockers must explain the reason

What changed:
Common RFQ disabled/failure paths now surface bilingual feedback instead of only `console.error`: copy/new-round RFQ creation, vendor decline / Keep Price, factory bulk offer upload, and add-product option-load failures.

Why:
Production/vendor users can be blocked by missing group names, missing `.xlsx` files, inactive merch groups, failed dropdown loads, or backend copy guards. Console-only errors are invisible in normal use, and disabled Material buttons often need a wrapper element for tooltips to fire.

Future sessions should:
For any new disabled RFQ action, add a tooltip that names the missing prerequisite. For any caught API failure that changes user workflow, keep the `console.error` but also show a concise snackbar. If the disabled element is a Material button, wrap it in an inline element with `matTooltip`.

### Async valueSetters

Every valueSetter that calls `recalculate_Lic_Gen` is `async`. AG Grid v35 awaits the returned Promise — a rejection cancels the cell edit and reverts the value. Always:
- Return `true` on success
- Return `false` and show a snackbar on validation failure
- Do not let any exception escape (the try-catch in `recalculate_Lic_Gen` itself covers margin errors, but the outer setter must also be safe)

### Fill handle: keep drag-copy, block double-click fill-down

What changed:
The master RFQ grid still enables AG Grid fill-handle drag-copy through `cellSelection.handle: { mode: 'fill', direction: 'y', setFillValue }`. `rfq.component.ts` also installs `installFillHandleDoubleClickBlocker()` on grid ready, which catches `dblclick` events on `.ag-fill-handle` during the capture phase and stops AG Grid from auto-filling down the grid.

Why:
Double-clicking the selected-cell fill handle copied a Tech Pack Link value down the displayed grid and destroyed many row values. Users still need normal drag-to-copy, so removing the fill handle is not acceptable.

Future sessions should:
Do not "fix" this by setting `suppressFillHandle: true` globally or removing `cellSelection.handle`; that breaks drag-copy. AG Grid v35.1 docs expose the fill handle as an on/off feature and do not document a narrower double-click-only setting, so keep the event blocker unless AG Grid adds a first-class option. Verify with `rfq.component.spec.ts` tests for drag fill and double-click blocking.

### Tech Pack Link editing and opening

What changed:
The Tech Pack Link column renders through `renderTechPackCell()` instead of returning a raw `<a>` string. The cell shows the URL text plus explicit `Edit`, `Open`, and `Upload` buttons; normal clicks no longer navigate away.

Why:
When the whole cell was an anchor, users could not reliably select/edit/delete URL text because clicking the cell opened a new browser tab. The explicit buttons keep opening/uploading available while leaving AG Grid text editing accessible via double-click or the `Edit` button.

Future sessions should:
Keep Tech Pack Link navigation behind an explicit control. If changing this renderer, verify that users can still edit/delete the text value and that opening the link requires clicking `Open`.

### Native AG Grid undo

What changed:
RFQ uses AG Grid's native undo stack (`undoRedoCellEditing: true`, limit `50`), and the toolbar Undo button calls `gridApi.undoCellEditing()`. The old custom undo path that fetched all RFQ rows on every edit was removed.

Why:
AG Grid records edits, fill-handle operations, paste, delete, undo, and redo through `cellValueChanged`; the custom snapshot restore did not reliably cover fill/copy/paste and could restore stale server data.

Future sessions should:
Keep persistence in `onCellValueChanged` so undo operations save the reverted row. If adding custom edit flows, make sure they use normal AG Grid value changes (`setDataValue`, valueSetter, or editor commit) so the undo stack can see them.

### Column identity: never hardcode field names

```typescript
// Correct
params.data[this.getMasterFiledName('cbm_pc')]

// Wrong
params.data['CBM/pc']
```

### Duty rate: always use optional chaining

The duty rate can be stored as `{ title: "7.5" }` (object from autocomplete) or as a raw numeric string. Always:

```typescript
const dutyRateRaw = data[this.getMasterFiledName('duty_rate')];
const duty_rate = dutyRateRaw?.title ?? dutyRateRaw ?? 0;
```

### cellDataType: false on numeric string columns

Any column that uses `numericCellEditor` AND whose backend data is stored as strings **must** have `cellDataType: false` in its runtime setup block. Without it, AG Grid v35 infers the column type as `'text'` and issues warning `#135`, silently reverting the edit before the valueSetter is called.

```typescript
} else if (colHeaders[i].colId == "cbm_pc") {
  colHeaders[i].cellDataType = false;        // ← prevents warning #135
  colHeaders[i]['cellEditor'] = 'numericCellEditor';
  colHeaders[i].valueSetter = async (params) => { ... };
}
```

Do NOT use `cellDataType: 'number'` — that applies AG Grid's built-in number formatter and shows "invalid number" for string-valued cells loaded from the backend.

The `numericCellEditor` already enforces number-only input via `getValue()` returning `null` for any non-parseable value, so `cellDataType: false` does not open the door to bad data.

### AG Grid deprecated APIs — what NOT to use

| Removed | Replacement | Removed in |
|---|---|---|
| `params.column.userProvidedColDef` | `params.column.getColDef()` | AG Grid v35 |
| `params.columnApi` | `params.api` | AG Grid v31 |
| `getRowNodeId` | `getRowId` | AG Grid v28 |
| `observable.toPromise()` | `firstValueFrom(observable)` | RxJS 7 (deprecated) |

---

## Detail grid (vendor quotes)

Each RFQ item row expands to show vendor quotes in a detail grid managed by `detail-cell-renderer.component.ts`. The detail grid:

- Loads its own column definitions from the backend (`getGridHeader({ grid_id: 'rfq_detail' })`)
- Has its own `onCellValueChanged` that calls `updateVendor(data)` on every change
- Uses `addDetailGridInfo` / `removeDetailGridInfo` for master-detail grid registration
- The `price_code` column change in the detail grid sets `quote_accepted = true` on the vendor row
- Default-sorts by factory price ascending — see "Default sort" below

### Default sort: lowest price first, unpriced/declined last

`configureDetailColumnHeaders()` calls `applyDefaultPriceSort(colHeaders)` after building the
column defs. This makes the **price column the primary default sort** (`sort: 'asc'`,
`sortIndex: 0`); the `name` column stays as the secondary alphabetical tiebreaker (`sortIndex: 1`).

A custom comparator on the price column ranks rows so that any quote with no usable price
(blank / zero / negative `Price`) or where the factory **declined to quote** (`req_status == 1`,
set by the "pass" button in `rfq-button.component.ts`) is sunk to the bottom regardless of value;
real quotes are ordered lowest-price-first above them. The comparator reads `node.data.Price`
directly (via `isUnpricedOrDeclined()` / `toPrice()`), so it does not depend on the column's
`colId`, `field`, or `valueGetter`.

**Why `applyDefaultPriceSort` searches for the colId instead of hardcoding it:** the backend
`rfq_detail` layout serves the visible price column under colId **`price`** (legacy), while the
config file `rfq.detail.grid.config.ts` uses `quote_price`. The helper targets whichever of the
two the layout actually provides, preferring the visible one. The first attempt (`e8ba0551`)
hardcoded `quote_price` and silently did nothing because the live column is `price`; fixed in
`b241ce79`. See `AGENTS.md §11 → "Detail-grid default sort targets the live price colId, not a hardcoded one"`.

### Vendor quote rows and the duplicate-factory guard

Each item's quotes live on the master row's **`Vendors`** array (capital V — Sequelize association name); each element is `{ Vendor: { id, ... }, Price, Note, Status, quote_id, ... }`. The same `Vendor.id` may appear more than once:

- **Allowed (factory side):** the detail sub-grid "Add Quote" button lets a factory submit a second quote line for the item, as long as a note explains why the price differs. This path is intentionally **unguarded**.
- **Blocked (staff side):** the "Add Factory" → "Select Factories" dialog (`vendor/`) must not let sourcing/production/admin add a factory that is already on the item. `onBtnNewVendor()` builds `existingVendorIds` from `node.data.Vendors.map(v => v?.Vendor?.id)` and passes them as `DialogData.vendors`; the dialog renders those factories red and unclickable.

The guard is **UI-only** — there is no DB unique constraint or backend dedupe on `(RFQ item, vendor)`, and it depends on the exact capital-`V` `Vendors` casing. See `AGENTS.md §11 → "Same factory twice on one RFQ item"`.

### Re-quote a stale price (orange flag + Keep Price)

When a factory's price is old, internal Sourcing/admin can right-click the detail-grid price cell → **"Request new quote"** (`getContextMenuItems`, gated to `admin`/`sourcing_manager`/`production`). That calls `itemService.requestRequote({ quote_id, priceCode })`, which sets `RFQVendor.requote_requested = true` and emails the factory. The flag drives, in **both** the staff and vendor views:

- an **orange** price cell — `cellClassRules: { 'requote-requested-cell': … }`, added to **both** the `quote_price` and legacy `price` colId branches (the live layout may serve either — same reason as the default-sort helper above);
- a **bilingual tooltip** `报价 - Quoted <Month D YYYY>` via `tooltipValueGetter`, using the original `quote_date` (which `requestRequote` leaves untouched).

The vendor clears it two ways: typing a **new price** (the `quote_price` valueSetter sets `requote_requested = false` locally; backend clears it on save) or clicking the **保留价格 - Keep Price** button (`rfq-button.component.ts` `onKeepPrice()` → `itemService.keepPrice({ quote_id })`, which clears the flag and refreshes `quote_date` to today). Vendor-facing strings are inline `中文 - English` (no i18n framework — see `AGENTS.md §11`). The backend guards both mutating endpoints with `vendorMayModifyQuote` so a vendor can't act on another factory's quote via `quote_id`.

---

## Window globals (known tech debt)

`window['itemService']` references in toolbar callbacks were resolved in commit `40b3ac9` — the service is now captured at closure-creation time in `getContextMenuItems()`.

Remaining `window` globals in `rfq.component.ts` (`window['router']`, `window['gridApi']`, `window['base64Images']`, `window['togglePageToolbar']`) are used for inter-callback communication in contexts where Angular DI is not available. **Do not add new `window['...']` references.**

content.content:
1:# AGENTS.md — Designflow Frontend: Primary Developer Guide
7:## 1. What this project is
13:## 2. Multi-model AI note
23:## 2a. Documentation map: what to read for each task
49:## 3. Repository / package structure
94:## 4. The Prime Directive
111:## 4a. Branch, sync, test, and PR workflow
139:## 5. Core modification inventory
158:## 6. Decision tree
208:## 7. Task-to-file navigation map
236:## 8. Data model / custom objects
257:### Sample Tracking Customer and Carrier Quirks
275:## 9. Container inventory
287:## 10. What to ignore
308:## 11. Idiosyncratic decisions
310:### Angular project name is "vex"
322:### `postSortRows` instead of `aggFunc` for group-row ordering
334:### `getMasterFiledName` (typo preserved intentionally)

308	## 11. Idiosyncratic decisions
309	
310	### Angular project name is "vex"
311	
312	**Looks like:** The wrong project name is configured — `angular.json` names the project `vex`.
313	
314	**Actually:** Designflow was bootstrapped from the commercial Vex Angular template. The CLI project name `vex` is the template's name and has never been changed.
315	
316	**Why:** Renaming an Angular CLI project requires updating `angular.json`, `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, and several other files. The risk of breakage during rename outweighs the cosmetic benefit.
317	
318	**Do not change because:** Build scripts in `package.json` (`ng serve`, `ng build`) rely on the `vex` project name implicitly through Angular CLI defaults. Renaming without updating every reference would break the build.
319	
320	---
321	
322	### `postSortRows` instead of `aggFunc` for group-row ordering
323	
324	**Looks like:** Date Modified uses a custom comparator and `postSortRows` hook but no `aggFunc` — looks like incomplete sorting implementation.
325	
326	**Actually:** This is deliberate. Adding `aggFunc` to ANY column activates AG Grid Enterprise's full aggregation pipeline for all group rows, causing `params.data` to be `undefined` in every `valueGetter` and `valueSetter` callback on group rows.
327	
328	**Why:** Adding `aggFunc: 'max'` to Date Modified (commit `43db88d`) broke all landed-cost and margin calculations because group rows suddenly had no `params.data`. Removed in `0a2f4f7`.
329	
330	**Do not change because:** Restoring `aggFunc` on any column will silently break every calculation that reads `params.data` in a valueSetter.
331	
332	---
333	
334	### `getMasterFiledName` (typo preserved intentionally)
335	
336	**Looks like:** A typo — should be `getMasterFieldName`.
337	
338	**Actually:** The function is named `getMasterFiledName` (not `FieldName`) across `rfq.component.ts`, `equation.ts`, `detail-cell-renderer.component.ts`, and related files. It is the stable canonical function name used throughout the codebase.
339	
340	**Why:** Renaming it now would require touching dozens of call-sites across multiple files, with no functional benefit and real risk of missed replacements.
341	
342	**Do not change because:** Renaming half the occurrences would break the build; renaming all of them is a pure cosmetic refactor with zero risk benefit.
343	
344	---
345	
346	### Duty rate arrives as `{ title: string }` OR as a raw number
347	
348	**Looks like:** Inconsistent data handling — some places access `duty_rate.title`, others use the value directly.
349	
350	**Actually:** The duty rate field is stored on the row as an object `{ title: "7.5" }` when set via the autocomplete editor, but can also be a raw string or number in some data paths.
351	
352	**Why:** The backend returns it as an object with a label; older code accessed `.title` directly without guarding.
353	
354	**Do not change because:** The guard pattern `dutyRateRaw?.title ?? dutyRateRaw ?? 0` handles both forms. Changing to always-object or always-primitive would require a coordinated backend change.
355	
356	---
357	
358	### `window['itemService']` global references in rfq.component.ts
359	
360	**Looks like:** Anti-pattern — using the global `window` object to access an Angular service.
361	
362	**Actually:** Some AG Grid callback closures registered in `applyColumnDefinitions()` (which runs before the component is fully initialized) cannot close over `this` because the method that creates them is called from a context where `this` bindings differ.
363	
364	**Why:** This is a known tech-debt pattern from the original codebase. The `window['equation']`, `window['colHeaders']`, and `window['_snackBar']` globals inside `container_price` were cleaned up in commit `8fa013a`. The remaining `window['itemService']` references in toolbar button callbacks have the same root cause.
365	
366	**Do not change without understanding:** Moving these calls to `this.itemService` will throw `Cannot read property of undefined` if the closure is created before Angular DI is complete.
367	
368	---
369	
370	### `rfqEquation`, `prodEquation`, `licensingEquation` are separate injectable services
371	
372	**Looks like:** Unnecessary over-engineering — the calculation functions could live directly in the grid components.
373	
374	**Actually:** Each tracking module (RFQ, production, licensing) has its own `equation.ts` calculation service. This is intentional separation: the grid component handles AG Grid wiring, the equation service handles all business math.
375	
376	**Why:** The calculation chains (8 margin × 2 type = 16 setters) are called from multiple places (direct cell edits, bulk updates, container cost dialog). Keeping them in an injectable service allows them to be called from dialogs as well as the grid component.
377	
378	**Do not change because:** Inlining the math into the component would make it impossible to call from dialogs without circular dependencies.
379	
380	---
381	
382	### AG Grid `colId` (stable) vs `field` (display/storage)
383	
384	**Looks like:** Redundant — why have both `colId` and `field` on a column definition?
385	
386	**Actually:** In `rfq.grid.config.ts`, `field` is the human-readable storage key (e.g. `"CBM/pc"`, `"FOB Cost"`, `"Landed Cost (LDP port)"`). `colId` is the lowercase-underscore stable identifier (`"cbm_pc"`, `"fob_cost"`, `"landed_cost"`). All code that looks up a column by id uses `getMasterFiledName(colId)` to get the field name, then uses the field name to access `params.data[field]`.
387	
388	**Why:** AG Grid requires `field` for data binding; the codebase uses `colId` as the stable internal identifier for all programmatic access.
389	
390	**Do not change because:** Changing `field` names would break data read/write on every row. Changing `colId` values would break all `getMasterFiledName` lookups.
391	
392	---
393	
394	### Duty rate is stored twice: equation AND evaluated total
395	
396	**Looks like:** Redundant storage — the cell has both an equation string (`"5.3+25+10"`) and an evaluated total (`40.3`).
397	
398	**Actually:** The grid cell displays the evaluated total (`40.3%`) so margin math in `equation.ts` can `parseFloat(duty_rate.title)`. The raw equation is stored separately so the user sees the breakdown when they click back into the cell (Excel-style).
399	
400	**Where it lives:**
401	- `[duty_rate field].title` — the evaluated total as a string. Consumed by all downstream calculations in `equation.ts`.
402	- `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` — the raw equation (DB column `VARCHAR(500)`). Persisted to the backend.
403	- `[duty_rate field]_equation` — ephemeral mirror that `MathExprCellEditor` reads on edit. Forward-synced from `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` in the cell renderer; reverse-synced back in the column's `valueSetter`.
404	
405	**Why:** The duty rate is a sum of three components (MFN + Section 301 + Trump 2025) and that breakdown is auditable information. Storing only the evaluated total destroys it. Storing only the equation forces every downstream consumer to re-evaluate on every read.
406	
407	**Do not change because:** Removing `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` reverts users to opaque single-number duty rates — they lose the ability to see what made up the 40.3% after the fact. Removing `.title` requires re-evaluating the equation in `equation.ts` on every margin calculation (and `equation.ts` runs hot — 16 margin setters per row edit).
408	
409	---
410	
411	### `async valueSetter` with Promise rejection cancels the cell edit (AG Grid v35)
412	
413	**Looks like:** A bug — the cell edit sometimes cancels even when the user typed a valid value.
414	
415	**Actually:** AG Grid v35 awaits an async `valueSetter`'s returned Promise. A rejected Promise (caused by any uncaught throw inside the setter) cancels the edit and reverts the cell to its previous value.
416	
417	**Why:** `equation.ts` previously accessed `params.column.userProvidedColDef.colId` (removed in AG Grid v35) inside every margin setter, causing a `TypeError` on every edit. Fixed in `daf2bf9`.
418	
419	**Do not change because:** Any uncaught exception inside an async valueSetter will silently revert the cell. All new valueSetters must be wrapped in try-catch, and all AG Grid API calls inside them must use current (non-deprecated) APIs.
420	
421	---
422	
423	### RFQ fill handle keeps drag-copy but blocks double-click fill-down
424	
425	**Looks like:** The RFQ grid still enables AG Grid's fill handle (`cellSelection.handle.mode = 'fill'`), even though double-clicking that blue handle previously copied a Tech Pack Link down the whole grid.
426	
427	**Actually:** Drag-to-copy is intentional and must remain. The dangerous path is only the AG Grid double-click shortcut on `.ag-fill-handle`, which auto-fills down the displayed grid range. AG Grid v35.1 docs expose fill-handle on/off via `cellSelection.handle`, but no documented option to disable only the double-click shortcut.
428	
429	**Why:** A designer accidentally double-clicked the Tech Pack Link fill handle and destroyed values down the grid. The fix in `rfq.component.ts` keeps drag fill enabled and installs a capture-phase `dblclick` listener that blocks events whose target is inside `.ag-fill-handle`.
430	
431	**Future sessions should:** Do not set `suppressFillHandle: true` globally or remove `cellSelection.handle` just to stop double-click fill-down; that also breaks desired drag-to-copy. Verify with `rfq.component.spec.ts` tests for drag-fill config and the double-click blocker.
432	
433	---
434	
435	### "Price for Sales" cells use a native-title tooltip via a cell renderer, not the shared `customTooltip`
436	
437	**Looks like:** Inconsistent — the 8 `*_price_sales` columns render through `priceSalesSnapshotRenderer` and set a plain browser `title`, while every other column uses the styled `CustomTooltipComponent`.
438	
439	**Actually:** Those 8 columns must show their duty-rate/container-price snapshot tooltip **always on hover**. The grid sets `tooltipShowMode: 'whenTruncated'` globally, and AG Grid has **no per-column override** for that mode.
440	
441	**Why:** Switching the grid to `'standard'` would change tooltip behavior for every column and the detail grid, and would require reworking the shared `CustomTooltipComponent` truncation gating (high regression risk per the AG Grid incidents in §14). A native `title` set by a dedicated renderer always shows on hover for just these 8 columns, fully isolated. The renderer also clears `tooltipField` on those columns so the AG tooltip does not double up.
442	
443	**Do not change because:** Reverting to the shared tooltip silently re-gates these tooltips behind truncation (they would rarely show, since the values are short currency strings). Capture lives in `onCellValueChanged` → `capturePriceSalesSnapshot()`; rendering in `price-sales-snapshot-renderer/`.
444	
445	---
446	
447	### Angular 21 defaults `standalone: true` — module-declared components need `standalone: false`
448	
449	**Looks like:** A new `@Component` decorator without `standalone` works in Angular 15 but silently fails in Angular 21 when declared in an `NgModule`.
450	
451	**Actually:** Angular 21 changed the `@Component` decorator default from `standalone: false` (Angular 14–16) to `standalone: true`. Any component you add to an `NgModule`'s `declarations` array will throw `NG0302: 'app-xyz' is not a known element` at runtime unless `standalone: false` is explicitly set.
452	
453	**Why:** The entire app is still module-based (not standalone). We have not migrated `AppModule`, `RFQModule`, etc. to standalone mode.
454	
455	**Do not change because:** Removing `standalone: false` from a module-declared component will break the module at runtime. Add it to every new `@Component` in this codebase until a full standalone migration is planned and coordinated. Example: `FactoryBulkUploadDialogComponent` required `standalone: false`.
456	
457	---
458	
459	### Vendor-facing UI strings are hardcoded bilingual (`中文 - English`), not i18n keys
460	
461	**Looks like:** Untranslated, hardcoded strings like `我不想给价格 - Decline`, `保留价格 - Keep Price`, and the `报价 - Quoted <date>` re-quote tooltip — looks like someone forgot to use the translation framework.
462	
463	**Actually:** `@ngx-translate/core` is installed but **not wired** (no `assets/i18n/*.json`, no `TranslateService` init). The vendor portal is used by Chinese factories, so the handful of vendor-facing strings are written inline as `中文 - English` directly in the templates/colDefs. This is the established pattern (`rfq-button.component.html` shipped the Decline button this way first).
464	
465	**Do not change because:** "Fixing" one string to a `| translate` key while the framework is uninitialized renders the raw key (e.g. `RFQ.DECLINE`). Either keep the inline bilingual form, or stand up the full ngx-translate pipeline in one coordinated change — don't half-migrate. New vendor-facing copy should follow the `中文 - English` convention.
466	
467	---
468	
469	### Same factory twice on one RFQ item: allowed for the factory, blocked for staff
470	
471	**Looks like:** Nothing stops the same factory being attached to an RFQ item twice — there is no DB unique constraint on `(RFQitem, vendor)` and no backend duplicate check, and the detail sub-grid even has an "Add Quote" button that adds a second row for a factory that is already present.
472	
473	**Actually:** Two flows with deliberately opposite rules. A **factory** may legitimately submit a second quote line for the same item (different price/MOQ, with a note explaining the difference) via "Add Quote" — that is real data and is allowed. **Staff** (sourcing/production/admin) must NOT add the same factory twice via the "Add Factory" → "Select Factories" dialog. The dialog (`src/app/pages/rfq/vendor/vendor.component.ts`) renders already-attached factories as red, unclickable chips: `toggleSelection()` early-returns for attached IDs, the group "All" checkbox skips them, and the chips get the `vendor-already-added` class. The attached IDs are seeded from `DialogData.vendors`, which `detail-cell-renderer.onBtnNewVendor()` builds from `node.data.Vendors.map(v => v?.Vendor?.id)`.
474	
475	**Why:** A factory quoting twice is a valid business event; staff adding the same factory twice is an accident that produces confusing duplicate quote rows.
476	
477	**Do not change because:** The guard is **UI-only** — there is no DB or backend constraint behind it, so the Select Factories dialog is the only thing preventing accidental staff-side duplicates. It depends on the master row property being capital `Vendors` with elements shaped `{ Vendor: { id } }`; changing that casing (e.g. to `vendors`) makes `existingVendorIds` always empty and silently disables the guard. The factory-side "Add Quote" path must stay unguarded.
478	
479	---
480	
481	### Detail-grid default sort targets the live price colId, not a hardcoded one
482	
483	**Looks like:** `applyDefaultPriceSort()` in `detail-cell-renderer.component.ts` searches `colHeaders` for a column whose `colId` is `'quote_price'` **or** `'price'` (preferring the visible one) before wiring the default sort — looks like defensive over-engineering that could be simplified to a single hardcoded colId.
484	
485	**Actually:** The detail grid's columns come from the backend `GridLayout` table (`getGridHeader({ grid_id: 'rfq_detail' })`), not from code. The **live `rfq_detail` layout serves the visible price column under colId `price`** (legacy), while the in-repo config `rfq.detail.grid.config.ts` uses `quote_price`. The two disagree, so the helper must match whichever the layout actually provides. The comparator also reads `node.data.Price` directly (via `isUnpricedOrDeclined()` / `toPrice()`) rather than the cell value, so it is independent of colId/field/valueGetter.
486	
487	**Why:** The first implementation (`e8ba0551`) set `sort`/`sortIndex`/`comparator` only on colId `quote_price`. At runtime that column does not exist (the live one is `price`), so the sort silently did nothing and the `Name` column kept owning the sort. Generalized to match either colId in `b241ce79`.
488	
489	**Do not change because:** Hardcoding a single colId reintroduces the silent no-op — the sort lands on a column that isn't on screen and `Name` wins. Until the detail grid is migrated to the static `rfq.detail.grid.config.ts` (config-migration is noted as a TODO in `detail-cell-renderer.component.ts`), the backend colId is the source of truth and may be `price`. The bottom-sink rule depends on `req_status == 1` meaning "declined" (see §8).
490	
491	---
492	
493	## 12. Credentials and environment
494	
495	All API base URLs are compiled into the Angular bundle at build time via `src/environments/`. There are no runtime `.env` files in the frontend container.
496	
497	| Variable | Purpose | Where it lives | Required in dev | Required in prod |

## 11. Idiosyncratic decisions

### Angular project name is "vex"

**Looks like:** The wrong project name is configured — `angular.json` names the project `vex`.

**Actually:** Designflow was bootstrapped from the commercial Vex Angular template. The CLI project name `vex` is the template's name and has never been changed.

**Why:** Renaming an Angular CLI project requires updating `angular.json`, `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, and several other files. The risk of breakage during rename outweighs the cosmetic benefit.

**Do not change because:** Build scripts in `package.json` (`ng serve`, `ng build`) rely on the `vex` project name implicitly through Angular CLI defaults. Renaming without updating every reference would break the build.

---

### `postSortRows` instead of `aggFunc` for group-row ordering

**Looks like:** Date Modified uses a custom comparator and `postSortRows` hook but no `aggFunc` — looks like incomplete sorting implementation.

**Actually:** This is deliberate. Adding `aggFunc` to ANY column activates AG Grid Enterprise's full aggregation pipeline for all group rows, causing `params.data` to be `undefined` in every `valueGetter` and `valueSetter` callback on group rows.

**Why:** Adding `aggFunc: 'max'` to Date Modified (commit `43db88d`) broke all landed-cost and margin calculations because group rows suddenly had no `params.data`. Removed in `0a2f4f7`.

**Do not change because:** Restoring `aggFunc` on any column will silently break every calculation that reads `params.data` in a valueSetter.

---

### `getMasterFiledName` (typo preserved intentionally)

**Looks like:** A typo — should be `getMasterFieldName`.

**Actually:** The function is named `getMasterFiledName` (not `FieldName`) across `rfq.component.ts`, `equation.ts`, `detail-cell-renderer.component.ts`, and related files. It is the stable canonical function name used throughout the codebase.

**Why:** Renaming it now would require touching dozens of call-sites across multiple files, with no functional benefit and real risk of missed replacements.

**Do not change because:** Renaming half the occurrences would break the build; renaming all of them is a pure cosmetic refactor with zero risk benefit.

---

### Duty rate arrives as `{ title: string }` OR as a raw number

**Looks like:** Inconsistent data handling — some places access `duty_rate.title`, others use the value directly.

**Actually:** The duty rate field is stored on the row as an object `{ title: "7.5" }` when set via the autocomplete editor, but can also be a raw string or number in some data paths.

**Why:** The backend returns it as an object with a label; older code accessed `.title` directly without guarding.

**Do not change because:** The guard pattern `dutyRateRaw?.title ?? dutyRateRaw ?? 0` handles both forms. Changing to always-object or always-primitive would require a coordinated backend change.

---

### `window['itemService']` global references in rfq.component.ts

**Looks like:** Anti-pattern — using the global `window` object to access an Angular service.

**Actually:** Some AG Grid callback closures registered in `applyColumnDefinitions()` (which runs before the component is fully initialized) cannot close over `this` because the method that creates them is called from a context where `this` bindings differ.

**Why:** This is a known tech-debt pattern from the original codebase. The `window['equation']`, `window['colHeaders']`, and `window['_snackBar']` globals inside `container_price` were cleaned up in commit `8fa013a`. The remaining `window['itemService']` references in toolbar button callbacks have the same root cause.

**Do not change without understanding:** Moving these calls to `this.itemService` will throw `Cannot read property of undefined` if the closure is created before Angular DI is complete.

---

### `rfqEquation`, `prodEquation`, `licensingEquation` are separate injectable services

**Looks like:** Unnecessary over-engineering — the calculation functions could live directly in the grid components.

**Actually:** Each tracking module (RFQ, production, licensing) has its own `equation.ts` calculation service. This is intentional separation: the grid component handles AG Grid wiring, the equation service handles all business math.

**Why:** The calculation chains (8 margin × 2 type = 16 setters) are called from multiple places (direct cell edits, bulk updates, container cost dialog). Keeping them in an injectable service allows them to be called from dialogs as well as the grid component.

**Do not change because:** Inlining the math into the component would make it impossible to call from dialogs without circular dependencies.

---

### AG Grid `colId` (stable) vs `field` (display/storage)

**Looks like:** Redundant — why have both `colId` and `field` on a column definition?

**Actually:** In `rfq.grid.config.ts`, `field` is the human-readable storage key (e.g. `"CBM/pc"`, `"FOB Cost"`, `"Landed Cost (LDP port)"`). `colId` is the lowercase-underscore stable identifier (`"cbm_pc"`, `"fob_cost"`, `"landed_cost"`). All code that looks up a column by id uses `getMasterFiledName(colId)` to get the field name, then uses the field name to access `params.data[field]`.

**Why:** AG Grid requires `field` for data binding; the codebase uses `colId` as the stable internal identifier for all programmatic access.

**Do not change because:** Changing `field` names would break data read/write on every row. Changing `colId` values would break all `getMasterFiledName` lookups.

---

### Duty rate is stored twice: equation AND evaluated total

**Looks like:** Redundant storage — the cell has both an equation string (`"5.3+25+10"`) and an evaluated total (`40.3`).

**Actually:** The grid cell displays the evaluated total (`40.3%`) so margin math in `equation.ts` can `parseFloat(duty_rate.title)`. The raw equation is stored separately so the user sees the breakdown when they click back into the cell (Excel-style).

**Where it lives:**
- `[duty_rate field].title` — the evaluated total as a string. Consumed by all downstream calculations in `equation.ts`.
- `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` — the raw equation (DB column `VARCHAR(500)`). Persisted to the backend.
- `[duty_rate field]_equation` — ephemeral mirror that `MathExprCellEditor` reads on edit. Forward-synced from `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` in the cell renderer; reverse-synced back in the column's `valueSetter`.

**Why:** The duty rate is a sum of three components (MFN + Section 301 + Trump 2025) and that breakdown is auditable information. Storing only the evaluated total destroys it. Storing only the equation forces every downstream consumer to re-evaluate on every read.

**Do not change because:** Removing `rfqItem_duty_rate_equation` reverts users to opaque single-number duty rates — they lose the ability to see what made up the 40.3% after the fact. Removing `.title` requires re-evaluating the equation in `equation.ts` on every margin calculation (and `equation.ts` runs hot — 16 margin setters per row edit).

---

### `async valueSetter` with Promise rejection cancels the cell edit (AG Grid v35)

**Looks like:** A bug — the cell edit sometimes cancels even when the user typed a valid value.

**Actually:** AG Grid v35 awaits an async `valueSetter`'s returned Promise. A rejected Promise (caused by any uncaught throw inside the setter) cancels the edit and reverts the cell to its previous value.

**Why:** `equation.ts` previously accessed `params.column.userProvidedColDef.colId` (removed in AG Grid v35) inside every margin setter, causing a `TypeError` on every edit. Fixed in `daf2bf9`.

**Do not change because:** Any uncaught exception inside an async valueSetter will silently revert the cell. All new valueSetters must be wrapped in try-catch, and all AG Grid API calls inside them must use current (non-deprecated) APIs.

---

### RFQ fill handle keeps drag-copy but blocks double-click fill-down

**Looks like:** The RFQ grid still enables AG Grid's fill handle (`cellSelection.handle.mode = 'fill'`), even though double-clicking that blue handle previously copied a Tech Pack Link down the whole grid.

**Actually:** Drag-to-copy is intentional and must remain. The dangerous path is only the AG Grid double-click shortcut on `.ag-fill-handle`, which auto-fills down the displayed grid range. AG Grid v35.1 docs expose fill-handle on/off via `cellSelection.handle`, but no documented option to disable only the double-click shortcut.

**Why:** A designer accidentally double-clicked the Tech Pack Link fill handle and destroyed values down the grid. The fix in `rfq.component.ts` keeps drag fill enabled and installs a capture-phase `dblclick` listener that blocks events whose target is inside `.ag-fill-handle`.

**Future sessions should:** Do not set `suppressFillHandle: true` globally or remove `cellSelection.handle` just to stop double-click fill-down; that also breaks desired drag-to-copy. Verify with `rfq.component.spec.ts` tests for drag-fill config and the double-click blocker.

---

### "Price for Sales" cells use a native-title tooltip via a cell renderer, not the shared `customTooltip`

**Looks like:** Inconsistent — the 8 `*_price_sales` columns render through `priceSalesSnapshotRenderer` and set a plain browser `title`, while every other column uses the styled `CustomTooltipComponent`.

**Actually:** Those 8 columns must show their duty-rate/container-price snapshot tooltip **always on hover**. The grid sets `tooltipShowMode: 'whenTruncated'` globally, and AG Grid has **no per-column override** for that mode.

**Why:** Switching the grid to `'standard'` would change tooltip behavior for every column and the detail grid, and would require reworking the shared `CustomTooltipComponent` truncation gating (high regression risk per the AG Grid incidents in §14). A native `title` set by a dedicated renderer always shows on hover for just these 8 columns, fully isolated. The renderer also clears `tooltipField` on those columns so the AG tooltip does not double up.

**Do not change because:** Reverting to the shared tooltip silently re-gates these tooltips behind truncation (they would rarely show, since the values are short currency strings). Capture lives in `onCellValueChanged` → `capturePriceSalesSnapshot()`; rendering in `price-sales-snapshot-renderer/`.

---

### Angular 21 defaults `standalone: true` — module-declared components need `standalone: false`

**Looks like:** A new `@Component` decorator without `standalone` works in Angular 15 but silently fails in Angular 21 when declared in an `NgModule`.

**Actually:** Angular 21 changed the `@Component` decorator default from `standalone: false` (Angular 14–16) to `standalone: true`. Any component you add to an `NgModule`'s `declarations` array will throw `NG0302: 'app-xyz' is not a known element` at runtime unless `standalone: false` is explicitly set.

**Why:** The entire app is still module-based (not standalone). We have not migrated `AppModule`, `RFQModule`, etc. to standalone mode.

**Do not change because:** Removing `standalone: false` from a module-declared component will break the module at runtime. Add it to every new `@Component` in this codebase until a full standalone migration is planned and coordinated. Example: `FactoryBulkUploadDialogComponent` required `standalone: false`.

---

### Vendor-facing UI strings are hardcoded bilingual (`中文 - English`), not i18n keys

**Looks like:** Untranslated, hardcoded strings like `我不想给价格 - Decline`, `保留价格 - Keep Price`, and the `报价 - Quoted <date>` re-quote tooltip — looks like someone forgot to use the translation framework.

**Actually:** `@ngx-translate/core` is installed but **not wired** (no `assets/i18n/*.json`, no `TranslateService` init). The vendor portal is used by Chinese factories, so the handful of vendor-facing strings are written inline as `中文 - English` directly in the templates/colDefs. This is the established pattern (`rfq-button.component.html` shipped the Decline button this way first).

**Do not change because:** "Fixing" one string to a `| translate` key while the framework is uninitialized renders the raw key (e.g. `RFQ.DECLINE`). Either keep the inline bilingual form, or stand up the full ngx-translate pipeline in one coordinated change — don't half-migrate. New vendor-facing copy should follow the `中文 - English` convention.

---

### Same factory twice on one RFQ item: allowed for the factory, blocked for staff

**Looks like:** Nothing stops the same factory being attached to an RFQ item twice — there is no DB unique constraint on `(RFQitem, vendor)` and no backend duplicate check, and the detail sub-grid even has an "Add Quote" button that adds a second row for a factory that is already present.

**Actually:** Two flows with deliberately opposite rules. A **factory** may legitimately submit a second quote line for the same item (different price/MOQ, with a note explaining the difference) via "Add Quote" — that is real data and is allowed. **Staff** (sourcing/production/admin) must NOT add the same factory twice via the "Add Factory" → "Select Factories" dialog. The dialog (`src/app/pages/rfq/vendor/vendor.component.ts`) renders already-attached factories as red, unclickable chips: `toggleSelection()` early-returns for attached IDs, the group "All" checkbox skips them, and the chips get the `vendor-already-added` class. The attached IDs are seeded from `DialogData.vendors`, which `detail-cell-renderer.onBtnNewVendor()` builds from `node.data.Vendors.map(v => v?.Vendor?.id)`.

**Why:** A factory quoting twice is a valid business event; staff adding the same factory twice is an accident that produces confusing duplicate quote rows.

**Do not change because:** The guard is **UI-only** — there is no DB or backend constraint behind it, so the Select Factories dialog is the only thing preventing accidental staff-side duplicates. It depends on the master row property being capital `Vendors` with elements shaped `{ Vendor: { id } }`; changing that casing (e.g. to `vendors`) makes `existingVendorIds` always empty and silently disables the guard. The factory-side "Add Quote" path must stay unguarded.

---

### Detail-grid default sort targets the live price colId, not a hardcoded one

**Looks like:** `applyDefaultPriceSort()` in `detail-cell-renderer.component.ts` searches `colHeaders` for a column whose `colId` is `'quote_price'` **or** `'price'` (preferring the visible one) before wiring the default sort — looks like defensive over-engineering that could be simplified to a single hardcoded colId.

**Actually:** The detail grid's columns come from the backend `GridLayout` table (`getGridHeader({ grid_id: 'rfq_detail' })`), not from code. The **live `rfq_detail` layout serves the visible price column under colId `price`** (legacy), while the in-repo config `rfq.detail.grid.config.ts` uses `quote_price`. The two disagree, so the helper must match whichever the layout actually provides. The comparator also reads `node.data.Price` directly (via `isUnpricedOrDeclined()` / `toPrice()`) rather than the cell value, so it is independent of colId/field/valueGetter.

**Why:** The first implementation (`e8ba0551`) set `sort`/`sortIndex`/`comparator` only on colId `quote_price`. At runtime that column does not exist (the live one is `price`), so the sort silently did nothing and the `Name` column kept owning the sort. Generalized to match either colId in `b241ce79`.

**Do not change because:** Hardcoding a single colId reintroduces the silent no-op — the sort lands on a column that isn't on screen and `Name` wins. Until the detail grid is migrated to the static `rfq.detail.grid.config.ts` (config-migration is noted as a TODO in `detail-cell-renderer.component.ts`), the backend colId is the source of truth and may be `price`. The bottom-sink rule depends on `req_status == 1` meaning "declined" (see §8).

---

## 12. Credentials and environment

All API base URLs are compiled into the Angular bundle at build time via `src/environments/`. There are no runtime `.env` files in the frontend container.

| Variable | Purpose | Where it lives | Required in dev | Required in prod |

content.text:
I've read everything: the handoff README, the prototype HTML + `rfq-grid.jsx` / `rfq-subgrid.jsx` design references, the repo `src/app/pages/rfq/README.md`, AGENTS.md §11, and the real files — `rfq.detail.grid.config.ts` (+spec), `rfq.grid.config.ts` (+spec), `rfq.component.ts` (gridOptions, `getMasterFiledName`, `getAllRFQItem` column pipeline, `onGridReady`/`onFirstDataRendered`), `rfq.component.html`/`.scss`, and `detail-cell-renderer.component.ts`.

A few important findings that shape the plan:

- The master grid's columns are built from `RFQ_COLUMN_HEADERS_CONFIG` → mutated in the big `getAllRFQItem` loop → `this.colHeaders` → `this.columnDefs` (bound in the template). `getMasterFiledName()` iterates `this.colHeaders` by `colId`, so **`this.colHeaders` must stay a flat array** — column groups have to be applied to `this.columnDefs` only, not `this.colHeaders`.
- The **live detail grid uses backend layout colHeaders** in `configureDetailColumnHeaders()`, which already implements a flex policy (`note` flex 2, `name` flex 1 + maxWidth, others maxWidth 120 + `autoSizeColumns`). `sizeColumnsToFit()` is already commented out (748). The static `rfq.detail.grid.config.ts` is **not currently wired to the live grid** and still has hard widths.
- `groupDefaultExpanded: 1` in gridOptions is for **row** grouping — I will **not** touch it (the handoff's suggestion to set it to 0 is wrong; column-group collapse is driven by `columnGroupShow`, not `groupDefaultExpanded`).
- The `group-header-gen-*/lic-*` classes are referenced on colDefs but I found no CSS rules defining the tints yet — I'll add them in step 4.

## Implementation plan (4 PR-sized commits, in your order)

**Commit 1 — Detail sub-grid flex sizing** (`rfq.detail.grid.config.ts` + spec)
Apply the handoff §4 flex policy to the static config: `Note`/`vendor_note` → `flex: 1`, `minWidth: 240`, drop its hard width; give priced/lookup columns `minWidth` (Name 180, Price 110, Lead 96, etc.); keep `Name` pinned-left and the existing hidden columns. Add spec assertions (note has flex + no fixed width, fixed columns have minWidth, count stays 10). No `sizeColumnsToFit` added. *Note: this aligns the config to the handoff but won't change on-screen behavior, since the live grid reads backend layout — flagged below.*

**Commit 2 — Density toggle** (`rfq.component.ts` + `.html`, scss for the control)
Add `RFQ_DENSITY` (compact 30/30, standard 36/32, comfortable 44/36), `setDensity()` using `setGridOption('rowHeight'/'headerHeight')` + `resetRowHeights()`, persisted to `localStorage['rfq:density']`, read on grid-ready (default `standard`). Add a 3-segment toggle in the toolbar `utility-group`. Tests in `rfq.component.spec.ts` for persistence + API calls. *Caveat: `defaultColDef.autoHeaderHeight: true` means `headerHeight` is best-effort when headers wrap — row height is the reliable lever.*

**Commit 3 — Frozen identity + seam** (`rfq.grid.config.ts` + `rfq.component.scss`)

**Query:** setGridOption rowHeight headerHeight dynamic change resetRowHeights and autoHeaderHeight interaction
**Version:** 35.1.0
**Framework:** angular
**Total Results:** 10

---

Setting the row height is done once for each row. Once set, the grid will not ask you for the row height again. You can change the row height after it is initially set using a combination of `api.resetRowHeights()`, `rowNode.setRowHeight(height)` and `api.onRowHeightChanged()`.


### api.resetRowHeights()


Call this API to have the grid clear all the row heights and work them all out again from scratch - if you provide a `getRowHeight(params)` callback, it will be called again for each row. The grid will then resize and reposition all rows again.


### resetRowHeights

```typescript
/**
 * Tells the grid to recalculate the row heights.
 * 
 * @agModule `ClientSideRowModelApiModule / ServerSideRowModelApiModule`
 */
() => void
```


### rowNode.setRowHeight(height) and api.onRowHeightChanged()


You can call `rowNode.setRowHeight(height)` directly on the row node to set its height. The grid will resize the row but will NOT reposition the rows (i.e. if you make a row shorter, a space will appear between it and the next row - the next row will not be moved up). When you have set the row height (potentially on many rows) you need to call `api.onRowHeightChanged()` to tell the grid to reposition the rows. It is intended that you can call `rowNode.setRowHeight(height)` many times and then call `api.onRowHeightChanged()` once at the end.


When calling `rowNode.setRowHeight(height)`, you can either pass in a new height, or `null` or `undefined`. If you pass a height, that height will be used for the row. If you pass in `null` or `undefined`, the grid will then calculate the row height in the usual way, either using the provided `rowHeight` property or `getRowHeight(params)` callb

content.text:
All four steps are done, each as its own PR-sized commit, with the full RFQ spec suite green (96 tests across the 3 spec files) and a clean `tsc` typecheck after every grid-touching step.

## Summary

| # | Commit | What changed |
|---|--------|--------------|
| 1 | `3b090fc8` | **Detail sub-grid flex** — Note is the sole flexing column (`flex:1` + `minWidth`, no hard width); priced/lookup columns get minWidth floors and right-alignment; Price next to Name. Applied to both the static `rfq.detail.grid.config.ts` **and** the live `configureDetailColumnHeaders()` per your decision. No `sizeColumnsToFit` (already off). |
| 2 | `2e215829` | **Density toggle** — `RFQ_DENSITY` presets + `setDensity()`/`applyDensity()`/`readStoredDensity()`, persisted to `localStorage['rfq:density']`, restored on grid ready; 3-segment `mat-button-toggle` in the toolbar (`MatButtonToggleModule` wired). |
| 3 | `e5f81418` | **Frozen identity + seam** — `pic`/`desc` pinned left at the design widths (56 / 280); seam (rule + shadow) on the last pinned-left header/cell; brand pink-soft active-row highlight spanning the seam. |
| 4 | `eb7880ce` | **Collapsible scenario groups** — `buildRfqScenarioColumnGroups()` wraps the Gen/Lic × FOB/POE/WHSE/MDDP leaves into groups (Price-for-Sales always visible; Sell/Margin/Royalty `columnGroupShow:'open'`; `marryChildren`); toolbar expand/collapse-all via `setColumnGroupOpened`; group-band header tints. |

## Constraints honored
- All row-data access still goes through `getMasterFiledName()`; **`this.colHeaders` stays a flat array** — grouping is layered onto `this.columnDefs` only, so the lookup keeps working.
- Did **not** touch the fill-handle double-click blocker, native undo, or the detail default price sort.
- No column has both `flex` and `sizeColumnsToFit`.

## Two things to flag
- **`groupDefaultExpanded`** was deliberately **not** set to 0 (the handoff's suggestion). That option controls *row* grouping (currently `1`); column-group collapse is driven correctly by `columnGroupShow`, and groups already default collapsed.
- **Group open/closed state is session-only.** Persisting it inside named-view layouts (`layout-dropdown`, via `getColumnGroupState`/`setColumnGroupState`) is a deliberate follow-up — it touches the view-save path and I kept step 4 contained. Say the word and I'll add it.

I have **not pushed** — the commits are local on `sandbox-albert`. Want me to push and open/update the PR to `develop`? These are also only verified via tests + typecheck; I haven't run the app in a browser to eyeball the seam/groups/density visually.

https://github.com/popcre/designflow-frontend/pull/124
----- BODY -----
## Summary

Documentation-only. Records durable knowledge from a session that (a) analyzed how DesignFlow's `licensing_tracking` module overlaps with the **poppim** PM system, and (b) hit a phantom test failure worth flagging for future sessions.

## What's in here

- **`docs/licensing-tracking-poppim-integration.md` (new)** — `licensing_tracking` vs poppim overlap; the two systems run on separate clouds/DBs with no existing integration; chosen direction: **DesignFlow stays the system of record for licensing approval, poppim consumes a read-only copy**; and the shared-key finding — the join key is the DesignFlow `item_num_id` **embedded in poppim's `product.name`** (not `product.code`, a ClickUp slug). 9,293 distinct item-nos across 58% of 16,534 poppim rows.
- **`AGENTS.md`** — §14 incident-log entry: phantom `rfq.component.spec` TS errors (`notesDataSource`/`refreshNotes`/`NotesDataSource` "do not exist") were **stale `node_modules`** after a `develop` merge bumped AG Grid 35.2.1 → 35.3; fix is `yarn install`, not a code change. Plus doc-map + pending-work rows.
- **`HANDOFF.md`** — shared-key verification handoff: **blocked on a read-only DesignFlow DB export** from the PLM developer; includes the exact queries (A/B/C) and the next action (intersect with the poppim item-nos for a go/no-go).

## Verification

- Docs derived from verified session findings (CSV export measured; test/build results reproduced).
- No secrets, tokens, or credentials added.
- No source/behavior changes.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

- No column has both `flex` and `sizeColumnsToFit`.

### Notes / follow-ups
- `groupDefaultExpanded` intentionally left as-is (that controls *row* grouping; column-group collapse is handled by `columnGroupShow`).
- Column-group open/closed state is session-only; persisting it in named-view layouts (`layout-dropdown`) is a deliberate follow-up.
- Verified via tests + typecheck; not yet eyeballed in a running browser.

---

## (Earlier in this branch) poppim PM integration analysis + AG Grid 35.3 deps gotcha

Documentation-only. Records durable knowledge from a session that (a) analyzed how DesignFlow's `licensing_tracking` module overlaps with the **poppim** PM system, and (b) hit a phantom test failure worth flagging for future sessions.

- **`docs/licensing-tracking-poppim-integration.md` (new)** — `licensing_tracking` vs poppim overlap; separate clouds/DBs, no existing integration; chosen direction: DesignFlow stays system of record, poppim consumes a read-only copy; shared key is the DesignFlow `item_num_id` embedded in poppim's `product.name`. 9,293 distinct item-nos across 58% of 16,534 poppim rows.
- **`AGENTS.md`** — §14 incident-log entry: phantom `rfq.component.spec` TS errors were stale `node_modules` after a `develop` merge bumped AG Grid 35.2.1 → 35.3; fix is `yarn install`.
- **`HANDOFF.md`** — shared-key verification handoff (blocked on a read-only DB export), with the exact A/B/C queries and next action.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
